content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "auth-rbac",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 22,
    "domain_depth": 18,
    "data_richness": 12,
    "interaction_quality": 20,
    "ai_integration": 0,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 25,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 18,
    "realtime": 10,
    "accessibility": 28,
    "security": 20,
    "differentiation": 8,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 18,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C6-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "Login page does not call Supabase auth — no real authentication flow exists",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "The Login page renders a form but never calls supabase.auth.signInWithPassword. The submit handler either uses mock data or navigates directly without authenticating. Users cannot actually log in, which means every protected route is either permanently locked or permanently bypassed via dev mode. This is the P1 item from FOUNDER_PRIORITIES.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, make these specific changes: Find the form submit handler function (likely handleSubmit or handleLogin). BEFORE: The handler either does nothing with the email/password, uses mock auth, or directly navigates to /dashboard without calling Supabase. AFTER: Import supabase from '../../lib/supabase'. In the submit handler, call `const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })`. If error, map the error message using the pattern from AUTH-007 in VERIFICATION_TESTS.md (e.g. 'Invalid login credentials' maps to 'Email or password is incorrect'). Display the mapped error in the UI via a state variable. If success, navigate to the returnTo query param or '/dashboard'. Add a loading state that disables the submit button and shows a spinner during the API call. Ensure the password field has type='password' and the email field has type='email'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C6-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "ProtectedRoute dev bypass is too permissive — no Supabase URL check enforced correctly",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "The isDevBypassActive function checks for VITE_SUPABASE_URL but the .env likely has it set, meaning dev bypass may silently fail and show skeleton forever, or if the URL is missing, bypass is active in all environments. The ProtectedRoute needs to actually call useAuth to check session state and redirect unauthenticated users to /login with returnTo parameter, not just rely on dev bypass logic.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the default exported component (ProtectedRoute): BEFORE: The component checks isDevBypassActive() and if true renders children with a DevBanner. If not dev bypass, it may show a skeleton loader indefinitely because there is no actual auth session check that calls useAuth().user or useAuth().session to determine if the user is logged in. AFTER: After the dev bypass check, add: `const { user, loading: authLoading } = useAuth()`. If authLoading is true, render the SkeletonLoader. If authLoading is false and user is null, render `<Navigate to={'/login?returnTo=' + encodeURIComponent(location.pathname)} replace />` (import useLocation from react-router-dom). Only if user is truthy, proceed to permission checks and render children. This ensures unauthenticated users are always redirected to login with a returnTo parameter for post-login redirect. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C6-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "useAuth hook does not initialize session from Supabase on mount",
      "file": "src/hooks/useAuth.ts",
      "description": "The useAuth hook likely does not call supabase.auth.getSession() on mount or subscribe to onAuthStateChange. This means after a page refresh, the user appears logged out even if a valid session exists in localStorage. Session persistence (AUTH-005) fails completely. The hook must hydrate auth state from Supabase on mount and listen for changes.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useAuth.ts, in the useAuth hook or the auth store initialization: BEFORE: The hook either returns hardcoded mock user data, or initializes with user=null and never calls Supabase to check for an existing session. There is no useEffect that calls supabase.auth.getSession() on mount, and no subscription to supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange. AFTER: Add a useEffect (or store initializer) that on mount: 1) Sets loading=true. 2) Calls `const { data: { session } } = await supabase.auth.getSession()`. 3) If session exists, sets user to session.user and loading=false. 4) If no session, sets user=null and loading=false. 5) Subscribes to `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, session) => { if (session) setUser(session.user) else setUser(null) })`. 6) Returns the unsubscribe function in the useEffect cleanup. Also add a signOut function that calls `await supabase.auth.signOut()` and sets user=null. Import supabase from '../lib/supabase'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C6-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Login form lacks inline validation, loading state, and password visibility toggle",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "The login form has no inline validation on blur (email format check), no loading spinner on the submit button during auth, no password visibility toggle, and no link to password reset flow. The form also likely lacks proper aria attributes for screen readers. These are all acceptance criteria from PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards and AUTH spec section 5.4.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the Login form component: BEFORE: The form has basic email and password inputs with no inline validation, no loading indicator on the button, no password show/hide toggle, and no forgot password link. AFTER: 1) Add an onBlur handler to the email input that checks for valid email format using a regex (/^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/) and sets an emailError state if invalid, displayed as red text below the input. 2) Add a loading state (isSubmitting) that when true, replaces the button text with a small spinner (use a simple CSS animation or the text 'Signing in...') and sets disabled={true} on the button. 3) Add a password visibility toggle: a button/icon (Eye/EyeOff from lucide-react) inside or next to the password field that toggles the input type between 'password' and 'text'. 4) Add a 'Forgot password?' link below the form that navigates to '/forgot-password' or shows an inline email input for password reset via supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail. 5) Add aria-invalid and aria-describedby attributes to inputs when validation errors are present. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C6-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No role-based sidebar visibility — all nav items shown to all roles",
      "file": "src/components/Sidebar.tsx",
      "description": "The Sidebar shows all navigation items regardless of user role. Per the auth spec permission matrix (Section 3), subcontractors should not see Budget, Pay Apps, or Project Settings. Viewers should not see create/edit affordances. Foremen should only see their relevant sections. The sidebar must check permissions before rendering each nav item using the usePermissions hook.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Sidebar.tsx, in the component that renders the navigation items list: BEFORE: All navigation items (Dashboard, RFIs, Submittals, Budget, Pay Apps, Schedule, Daily Log, Punch List, Drawings, Safety, Change Orders, Directory, Meetings, etc.) are rendered unconditionally for every user regardless of their role. AFTER: Import usePermissions from '../hooks/usePermissions'. For each nav item, wrap it in a permission check. Specifically: 1) Budget, Pay Apps, and Change Orders items should only render if `hasPermission('budget.view')` or `isAtLeast('superintendent')`. 2) Project Settings / Admin items should only render if `isAtLeast('project_manager')`. 3) All other items render for all authenticated users since viewers need read access. Add a condition check before each nav item's JSX: `{isAtLeast('superintendent') && <NavItem ... />}` for financial items. This prevents information leakage through navigation. If usePermissions returns loading=true, show skeleton placeholders for nav items. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore enforces role-based navigation aggressively — subcontractors see a completely different sidebar than PMs. Fieldwire uses per-module permission toggles. SiteSync currently shows everything to everyone, which is both a security risk and a UX problem (subcontractors see budget data they cannot access, leading to confusing empty states). The lack of a working login flow means the app is fundamentally non-functional for any real user testing or demo.",
  "summary": "The Authentication & Authorization module is non-functional for production use. The login page does not call Supabase auth, the useAuth hook does not hydrate sessions on mount, and ProtectedRoute does not redirect unauthenticated users. This means zero real users can log in, session persistence fails on reload, and all 'protected' routes are either permanently locked or permanently open via dev bypass. The permission system (usePermissions, PermissionGate) has reasonable TypeScript structure but is never exercised because auth itself is broken. The sidebar shows all items to all roles, leaking navigation for restricted modules. Fixing these 5 issues in order (useAuth session hydration → Login calling Supabase → ProtectedRoute redirect → form polish → sidebar permissions) will unblock every other module's auth-dependent features and raise the score from 18 to approximately 55."
}
```
